comment: "I found the systematic approach to facial trauma assessment and management particularly valuable, especially the integration of anatomy, mechanism of injury, and surgical planning. The case-based discussions were especially enriching."

"This course offered a comprehensive yet systematic look at facial anatomy, fracture classification, and evidence-based management. The case-based approach clearly connected clinical findings with imaging, and the focus on interdisciplinary trauma care made it both intellectually engaging and clinically relevant.”

"Explaining procedures from real patient images made the topic more comprehensible and understandable for me. Even though I am not a specialist of plastic surgery and unfamiliar to those techniques, I could easily learn how to approach to a trauma patient."
